translations.descricao: Atopic dermatitis is a chronic inflammatory skin disease, characterized by intense itching and eczematous lesions. It mainly affects children, with global prevalence ranging from 15-20% in those under 5 years old. In Brazil, it is estimated to affect about 10-15% of children. Management involves hydration, topical corticosteroids, and in severe cases, immunosuppressants.
translations.sus.indicacao: Diagnosis and initial management in primary care for patients suspected of atopic dermatitis, with referral to a specialist in moderate to severe cases.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Children and adults with recurrent itching, eczematous lesions, and family history of atopy.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The American Academy of Dermatology (AAD 2023) recommends diagnosis based on clinical criteria and stepped management with emollients and topical anti-inflammatories.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: Global prevalence in children: 15-20%; in adults: 1-3%. In Brazil: 12% in urban children.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Global annual incidence: 10-15% in infants; in Brazil: approximately 8-10 cases per 1,000 children/year.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Low direct mortality, but associated with secondary infections (0.1-0.5% of severe cases); in Brazil, indirectly contributes to 0.2% of deaths from skin infections.
